model_narrative: We plan preventive maintenance for the parts installed across the sections of a plant over a horizon of consecutive months. For each section-part pair, each event type, and each month we decide whether to perform that maintenance event, and for each month we decide whether to take a plant shutdown. Performing a maintenance event costs a per-event amount that depends on the part and the event type, and every shutdown incurs a fixed cost. The objective is to minimize the total cost over the whole plan.

sets.name: L
sets.doc: the plant sections

sets.name: I
sets.doc: the maintenance event types that can be performed on a part

sets.name: J
sets.doc: the part numbers

sets.name: T
sets.doc: the planning months, given as consecutive integers in chronological order; the months are exactly the integers from 1 up to the total number of months, so a month index plus an offset can be compared against the count of months to tell whether a window runs past the end of the horizon

sets.name: K
sets.doc: the valid section-part pairs; each member is a (section, part) pair stating that the part is installed in that section, and only these pairs are maintained

params.name: f
params.doc: the maximum allowed number of months between successive maintenance events for each section-part pair and event type, given as a count of months; an event of that type must occur within any run of consecutive months of this length

params.name: c
params.doc: the cost of performing one maintenance event for each section-part pair and event type, in INR

params.name: w
params.doc: the pullback window used to consolidate shutdowns, given as a count of months; it bounds how close together in time two shutdowns may occur

params.name: oc
params.doc: the cost incurred each time the plant is shut down, in INR

params.name: m
params.doc: a big-M constant that upper-bounds how many maintenance events can be performed simultaneously in a single month; large enough to never bind when a shutdown is active, lower case purely due to naming rules

vars.name: x
vars.doc: one when event type i is performed on section-part pair (l,j) in month t, zero otherwise

vars.name: o
vars.doc: one when the plant is shut down in month t, zero otherwise

vars.name: tc
vars.doc: the total cost over the whole plan, in INR

constraints.name: f[l,
constraints.intent: Each part in a section must receive each type of maintenance event often enough that it is never neglected for too long. For every section-part pair and every event type there is a maximum number of consecutive months that may pass without that event. Looking at any month as the start of a window of that length, at least one event of that type must be scheduled on that pair somewhere within the window. When such a window would extend past the end of the planning horizon, no requirement is imposed for that starting month.

constraints.name: x[l,
constraints.intent: Maintenance can only happen in a month when the plant is shut down. For each month, if no shutdown is taken that month then no maintenance events of any type may be performed on any section-part pair, while if a shutdown is taken the number of events that month is allowed to be as large as needed. Express this by limiting the total number of maintenance events performed across all section-part pairs and event types in that month so that it can be positive only when the shutdown indicator for that month is on.

constraints.name: w)
constraints.intent: Shutdowns must be spread out in time and not bunched together. For any month taken as the start of a window whose length is the pullback window, at most one shutdown may occur within that window. When such a window would extend past the end of the planning horizon, no requirement is imposed for that starting month.

constraints.name: x[l,
constraints.intent: The total cost accounts for both maintenance and shutdowns. Add up, over every section-part pair, every event type, and every month, the per-event cost of each maintenance event that is performed, then add the fixed shutdown cost for every month in which a shutdown is taken. Set the total cost equal to this sum.

query: Operations is pitching an early-checkout idea: get the first plant outage onto the calendar within the first three months rather than waiting until month 4. We're evaluating it — if at least one shutdown has to fall in months 1 through 3, what total cost does the optimizer return?

query: The reliability team is floating a tighter cadence — they'd like to see more frequent outages, at least seven over the planning horizon. There's a proposal on the table to require seven or more shutdowns; plug that in and tell me the resulting cost.
expected_pyomo: model.min_outages = Constraint(expr=sum(model.o[t] for t in model.T) >= 7)

query: Operations is floating a front-loaded turnaround package: get three outages onto the calendar inside the first six months to clear the early backlog, and at the same time hold replacements to no more than thirty percent of all the maintenance jobs we book over the year so we're not over-swapping parts. There's a proposal on the table to run both of those together — what total cost does the optimizer return?
expected_pyomo:
model.front_outages = Constraint(expr=sum(model.o[t] for t in model.T if t <= 6) >= 3)
model.replace_cap = Constraint(expr=sum(model.x[l, j, 'Replace', t] for (l, j) in model.K for t in model.T) <= 0.3 * sum(model.x[l, j, i, t] for (l, j) in model.K for i in model.I for t in model.T))

query: Our maintenance bays get chaotic when an outage tries to cram too many jobs into one window — the crews start stepping on each other. Engineering is pitching a ceiling of fifteen maintenance events in any single month so each outage stays manageable. We're evaluating that across the whole horizon — what does the optimizer report for total cost?

query: Looking at the plan, the outages bunch up oddly — there's a stretch from month 8 to 14 with nothing on a clean rhythm. Wouldn't it be cleaner to just take the plant down every other month across that window, at 8, 10, 12 and 14? What's the model seeing that I'm not?

query: The plan leans awfully hard on full part replacements when a repair would often do — replacements are eating up a big slice of every outage. Shouldn't the schedule keep replacements down to at most a quarter of all the maintenance jobs we do over the year and rely on repairs for the rest? What's keeping the model from leaning that way?
expected_pyomo: model.replace_share = Constraint(expr=sum(model.x[l, j, 'Replace', t] for (l, j) in model.K for t in model.T) <= 0.25 * sum(model.x[l, j, i, t] for (l, j) in model.K for i in model.I for t in model.T))

query: The gaps between shutdowns look ragged — three months here, four there. Couldn't we just settle into a steady four-month rhythm, never letting two outages sit closer than four months apart? Walk me through what's pulling it the other way.

params.doc: the maximum allowed number of months between successive maintenance events for each section-part pair and event type, given as a count of months; an event of that type must occur within any run of consecutive months of this length

params.name: c
params.doc: the cost of performing one maintenance event for each section-part pair and event type, in INR

params.name: w
params.doc: the pullback window used to consolidate shutdowns, given as a count of months; it bounds how close together in time two shutdowns may occur

params.name: oc
params.doc: the cost incurred each time the plant is shut down, in INR

params.name: m
params.doc: a big-M constant that upper-bounds how many maintenance events can be performed simultaneously in a single month; large enough to never bind when a shutdown is active, lower case purely due to naming rules

vars.name: x
vars.doc: one when event type i is performed on section-part pair (l,j) in month t, zero otherwise

vars.name: o
vars.doc: one when the plant is shut down in month t, zero otherwise

vars.name: tc
vars.doc: the total cost over the whole plan, in INR

constraints.name: f[l,
constraints.intent: Each part in a section must receive each type of maintenance event often enough that it is never neglected for too long. For every section-part pair and every event type there is a maximum number of consecutive months that may pass without that event. Looking at any month as the start of a window of that length, at least one event of that type must be scheduled on that pair somewhere within the window. When such a window would extend past the end of the planning horizon, no requirement is imposed for that starting month.

constraints.name: x[l,
constraints.intent: Maintenance can only happen in a month when the plant is shut down. For each month, if no shutdown is taken that month then no maintenance events of any type may be performed on any section-part pair, while if a shutdown is taken the number of events that month is allowed to be as large as needed. Express this by limiting the total number of maintenance events performed across all section-part pairs and event types in that month so that it can be positive only when the shutdown indicator for that month is on.

constraints.name: w)
constraints.intent: Shutdowns must be spread out in time and not bunched together. For any month taken as the start of a window whose length is the pullback window, at most one shutdown may occur within that window. When such a window would extend past the end of the planning horizon, no requirement is imposed for that starting month.

constraints.name: x[l,
constraints.intent: The total cost accounts for both maintenance and shutdowns. Add up, over every section-part pair, every event type, and every month, the per-event cost of each maintenance event that is performed, then add the fixed shutdown cost for every month in which a shutdown is taken. Set the total cost equal to this sum.

query: We're swapping out part 4 in section 1 four separate times over the year, and those replacements are the priciest line items we have. Is there a reason the plan doesn't lean harder on repairs there and hold those replacements to no more than three? What's the tradeoff I'm missing?
expected_pyomo: model.cap_p4_replace = Constraint(expr=sum(model.x[1, 4, 'Replace', t] for t in model.T) <= 3)
